title: Review motion and animation comfort
question: Can users pause, reduce, or avoid motion that is not essential?

title: Review flashing and rapid visual change risk
question: Does any content flash more than three times per second, or could it be adjusted so it doesn't?

title: Review captions for audio/video content
question: Do all pre-recorded audio and video elements have accurate captions?

title: Review transcripts for media content
question: Is a full text transcript available for audio-only or video-only content?

title: Review keyboard-only usability
question: Can every interactive element and creative interaction be reached and operated using only a keyboard?

title: Review cognitive load
question: Is the interface free of unnecessary complexity, jargon, or steps that could overwhelm someone under time pressure or with a cognitive disability?

title: Review plain-language navigation
question: Are navigation labels, menu items, and instructions written in plain, unambiguous language?

title: Review canvas/sketch descriptions
question: Does the canvas or sketch have a text description that conveys what a sighted user would see?

title: Review sound-based interaction alternatives
question: Is sound-based feedback or interaction also available through a visual or text alternative?

title: Review touch target size
question: Are interactive touch targets at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels, or given adequate spacing if smaller?

title: Review consistent help placement
question: If help mechanisms exist across multiple pages, do they appear in the same relative order each time?

title: Review accessible authentication
question: Does authentication avoid relying solely on cognitive function tests such as memorizing passwords or solving puzzles, without an alternative?

title: Review multilingual accessibility notes
question: If the project serves multiple languages, are accessibility labels, alt text, and instructions translated and reviewed by fluent speakers?

title: Review bypass/skip navigation
question: Is there a way to skip repeated navigation blocks (a "skip to content" link, or landmark regions) so keyboard and screen reader users don't have to tab through the same menu on every page?

title: Review focus order
question: Does tabbing through the page move focus in an order that matches the visual reading order and preserves meaning?

title: Review focus visibility
question: Is there always a clearly visible focus indicator on the element that currently has keyboard focus, and is it never fully hidden behind other content?

title: Review consistent navigation
question: If navigation menus repeat across multiple pages, do they appear in the same relative order each time?

title: Review multiple ways to find content
question: Is there more than one way to locate a page within the site (e.g. navigation menu plus search, or a sitemap), other than a single required sequence of steps?
